key_files.relevance: handle_init method — where new _emit_state_changed(server, 'loading') must be inserted; currently no state emission before thread launch
_emit_state_changed implementation; 'loading' is a simple state requiring no extra params; acquires _state_lock then calls server.send
_init_backend background thread target; calls on_ready after initialize() completes; on_ready emits 'idle' at line 132
BackendState enum (LOADING='loading', IDLE, GENERATING, PAUSED, ERROR); StateChangedEvent dataclass (no extra fields for loading)
AppState initialization — backendState hardcoded to { state: 'loading' } at line 31-33; this is the value to remove/set to null
handleStateChanged: replaces state.backendState wholesale with payload; no merge; entry point for all state_changed events
updateLoadingOverlayForState: handles 'loading' as case; NOT called at startup, only from handleStateChanged — loading overlay relies on HTML/CSS defaults
TestInitCommand: existing handle_init tests; use patch.object(handler, '_init_backend') to suppress thread; none assert state emissions
TestEmitStateChanged: property-based tests for _emit_state_changed; line 570 tests simple states including 'loading'
createMockState() mirrors main.ts AppState init; hardcodes backendState.state='loading' at line 32; must be updated when main.ts changes
IPCServer.send: thread-safe stdout writer; used by _emit_state_changed; protected by _write_lock

patterns.name: Simple state emission
patterns.description: _emit_state_changed(server, 'loading') requires no prompt/message/fatal args; 'loading', 'idle', 'paused' are simple states that pass validation without extra fields

patterns.name: Mock server for unit tests
patterns.description: Tests use `server = Mock(); server.send = Mock()` then `call_args = mock_server.send.call_args[0][0]` to inspect sent messages

patterns.name: Patch _init_backend to isolate handle_init
patterns.description: All TestInitCommand tests use `patch.object(handler, '_init_backend')` to prevent background thread from running; assertions on synchronous part of handle_init only

patterns.name: Filter call_args_list by MessageType
patterns.description: state_changed_calls = [c for c in mock_server.send.call_args_list if c[0][0].type == MessageType.STATE_CHANGED]

patterns.name: Frontend full state replacement
patterns.description: handleStateChanged does state.backendState = payload — wholesale replacement. Frontend can safely start with null if updateLoadingOverlayForState is guarded against null.

similar_features.description: state_changed(idle) emission in on_ready callback after model loads — pattern for how loading -> idle transition currently works

TestEmitStateChanged/test_state_transitions_update_current_state already calls _emit_state_changed(server, BackendState.LOADING.value) and asserts _current_state updates — loading emission already tested in isolation

architecture.ipc_flow: handle_init (main thread) -> spawn _init_backend thread -> on_ready callback -> _emit_state_changed('idle') -> IPCServer.send -> stdout -> Tauri sidecar reader -> window.emit -> handleStateChanged -> state.backendState = payload
architecture.loading_gap: Currently no state is emitted between INIT receipt and 'idle' — the model loading window has no state event. Frontend hardcodes 'loading' to cover this gap.
architecture.thread_safety: _emit_state_changed called from main thread in handle_init before background thread starts — no concurrency concern. server.send uses _write_lock for thread-safe writes.

extension_points.description: Insert self._emit_state_changed(server, 'loading') after TextbrushBackend creation and before threading.Thread(...).start() — line 172 is the thread start
Change backendState: { state: 'loading' } to backendState: null — requires updating all guards that access state.backendState.state (isAlreadyGenerating at line 1008 is one such guard)
Update createMockState() to match main.ts change: backendState: null (or equivalent initial state)

testing.new_test_pattern: patch.object(handler, '_init_backend') then call handle_init, then filter mock_server.send.call_args_list for STATE_CHANGED with state='loading' as first call

conventions: Test classes named Test{Feature}Command or Test{BehaviorName}
Function-scoped fixtures; session-scoped only for real model loading
ruff linting required after each increment (CLAUDE.md)
BackendState enum values used as strings: BackendState.LOADING.value == 'loading'
All state emissions via _emit_state_changed, never direct server.send for state events
Frontend tests in src-tauri/ui/ co-located with source, use jsdom + node:test
